revision.comment: 新しいページ：兜塚古墳（奈良県桜井市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 兜塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]]浅古（字兜塚） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 53.87 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 8.18 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長40m
高さ3.5m（後円部・前方部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]（竪穴系横口式石室?）
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 装身具・鉄鏃・馬具・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 桜井市指定史跡「兜塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 兜塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''兜塚古墳'''（かぶとづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]浅古（あさご）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。桜井市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]}}{{small|阿蘇溶結[[凝灰岩]]製の古式家形石棺。}}]] [[奈良盆地]]南部、浅古集落西方の北西に延びる丘陵先端部に築造された古墳である。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は基壇上に築造されており、墳丘表面では[[葺石]]と見られる小礫のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]片が認められる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後円部中央における[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を墳丘主軸と平行方向とする{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。扁平な川原石積みで構築され{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}、長さ約3.7メートル（推定復元）・幅1.4メートルを測るが、現在までに大きく破壊されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石室の東側に礫敷が広がることから、竪穴系横口式石室とする説もある{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石室内には粘土が敷き詰められ、その上に阿蘇溶結[[凝灰岩]]（阿蘇ピンク石/馬門石）製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]が据えられる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石棺は長さ約2.1メートル・幅約1メートル・高さ約1メートルを測り、蓋石は稜線のほとんどない蒲鉾形で両側面に縄掛突起各2個がやや上向きに付くという古式家形石棺の様式になる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。盗掘に遭っているが、発掘調査では副葬品として装身具類（碧玉製管玉・琥珀製棗玉・銀製空玉・玻璃製小玉）・鉄鏃・馬具（金銅装鏡板・杏葉）が検出されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 古墳域は[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）に桜井市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：40メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：20メートル ** 高さ：3.5メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：19メートル ** 高さ：3.5メートル 墳丘は基壇の上に築造されており、基壇は東西48メートル・南北26メートル・高さ4メートルを測る{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 文化財 == === 桜井市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 兜塚古墳 - 2000年（平成12年）6月12日指定[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html 桜井の文化財]（桜井市立埋蔵文化財センター）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=清水眞一|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)|兜塚古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん さくらいし}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定史跡‎]]

（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 装身具・鉄鏃・馬具・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 桜井市指定史跡「兜塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 兜塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''兜塚古墳'''（かぶとづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]浅古（あさご）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。桜井市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]}}{{small|阿蘇溶結[[凝灰岩]]製の古式家形石棺。}}]] [[奈良盆地]]南部、浅古集落西方の北西に延びる丘陵先端部に築造された古墳である。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は基壇上に築造されており、墳丘表面では[[葺石]]と見られる小礫のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]片が認められる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。主体部の埋葬施設は後円部中央における[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を墳丘主軸と平行方向とする{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。扁平な川原石積みで構築され{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}、長さ約3.7メートル（推定復元）・幅1.4メートルを測るが、現在までに大きく破壊されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石室の東側に礫敷が広がることから、竪穴系横口式石室とする説もある{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石室内には粘土が敷き詰められ、その上に阿蘇溶結[[凝灰岩]]（[[阿蘇ピンク石]]/馬門石）製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]が据えられる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石棺は長さ約2.1メートル・幅約1メートル・高さ約1メートルを測り、蓋石は稜線のほとんどない蒲鉾形で両側面に縄掛突起各2個がやや上向きに付くという古式家形石棺の様式になる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。盗掘に遭っているが、発掘調査では副葬品として装身具類（碧玉製管玉・琥珀製棗玉・銀製空玉・玻璃製小玉）・鉄鏃・馬具（金銅装鏡板・杏葉）が検出されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 古墳域は[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）に桜井市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 構造 == 古墳の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：40メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：20メートル ** 高さ：3.5メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：19メートル ** 高さ：3.5メートル 墳丘は基壇の上に築造されており、基壇は東西48メートル・南北26メートル・高さ4メートルを測る{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 文化財 == === 桜井市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 兜塚古墳 - 2000年（平成12年）6月12日指定[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html 桜井の文化財]（桜井市立埋蔵文化財センター）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=清水眞一|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)|兜塚古墳}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん さくらいし}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定史跡]]

墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[桜井市]]浅古（字兜塚） |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 29|緯度秒 = 53.87 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 51|経度秒 = 8.18 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長40m
高さ3.5m（後円部・前方部） |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]（竪穴系横口式石室?）
（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = 装身具・鉄鏃・馬具・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 桜井市指定史跡「兜塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-西南西 |ラベル = 兜塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''兜塚古墳'''（かぶとづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]浅古（あさご）にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。桜井市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|後円部の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]}}{{small|阿蘇溶結[[凝灰岩]]製の古式家形石棺。}}]] [[奈良盆地]]南部、浅古集落西方の北西に延びる丘陵先端部に築造された古墳である。これまでに盗掘に遭っているほか、[[1954年]]（[[昭和]]29年）に発掘調査が実施されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向ける。墳丘は基壇上に築造されており、墳丘表面では[[葺石]]と見られる小礫のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]片が認められる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。埋葬施設は後円部中央における[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を墳丘主軸と平行方向とする{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。扁平な川原石積みで構築され{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}、長さ約3.7メートル（推定復元）・幅1.4メートルを測るが、現在までに大きく破壊されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。石室の東側に礫敷が広がることから、竪穴系横口式石室とする説もある{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石室内には粘土が敷き詰められ、その上に阿蘇溶結[[凝灰岩]]（[[阿蘇ピンク石]]/馬門石）製の刳抜式[[家形石棺]]が据えられる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。石棺は長さ約2.1メートル・幅約1メートル・高さ約1メートルを測り、蓋石は稜線のほとんどない蒲鉾形で両側面に縄掛突起各2個がやや上向きに付くという古式家形石棺の様式になる{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}（奈良盆地では同様の阿蘇溶結凝灰岩製の古式家形石棺の例として[[野神古墳]]（[[奈良市]]）が知られる）。盗掘に遭っているが、発掘調査では副葬品として装身具類（碧玉製管玉・琥珀製棗玉・銀製空玉・玻璃製小玉）・鉄鏃・馬具（金銅装鏡板・杏葉）が検出されている{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 古墳域は[[2000年]]（[[平成]]12年）に桜井市指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：40メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：20メートル ** 高さ：3.5メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：19メートル ** 高さ：3.5メートル 墳丘は基壇の上に築造されており、基壇は東西48メートル・南北26メートル・高さ4メートルを測る{{Sfn|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む == 文化財 == === 桜井市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 兜塚古墳 - 2000年（平成12年）6月12日指定[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html 桜井の文化財]（桜井市立埋蔵文化財センター）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=清水眞一|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|兜塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} * [[野神古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:かふとつかこふん さくらいし}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.text:
兜塚古墳 (桜井市)
[[Category:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]][[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} identified on the surface. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | Image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

[[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:竪穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:qq]]

Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: * Diameter: 20 meters * Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: * Width: 19 meters * Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | Coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Size = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]]

Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | Construction Period = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | Map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | Label = Kabutozuka Kofun | Label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]
