Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound Dimensions == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

revision.comment: more mass replacements to get the template working
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label Position = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Cultural Properties == === Sakurai City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Cultural Properties] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | Burial Facility = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]]

Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | Location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place Names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:bump]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | other_designation = Designated as a Sakurai City other_designation "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | Icon = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | other_designation = Designated as a Sakurai City other_designation "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is designated as a Sakurai City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a platform, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was designated as a Sakurai City other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a platform measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | other_other_designation = Designated as a Sakurai City other_other_designation "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated other_other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | other_other_designation = Designated as a Sakurai City other_other_designation "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_other_designation === * other_other_designation ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated other_other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | other_designation = Designated as a Sakurai City other_designation "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[en:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}{{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}} Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} inside | Excavated Items = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | built = Late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | other_designation = Designated as a Sakurai City other_designation "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|ja|桜井市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It has a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} design and is other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded {{ill|tuff|ja|凝灰岩}}, an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}}) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳}} in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the mid-{{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The site was other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == other_designation == === Sakurai City Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's other_designation] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami The site was other_designation1 as a Sakurai City other_designation in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height.
