With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JapanKnowledge|qq=}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|兜塚古墳 (桜井市)|version=91584033|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JapanKnowledge|qq=}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|兜塚古墳 (桜井市)|version=91584033|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Municipally Designated Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:奈良県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}}

Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JapanKnowledge|qq=}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|兜塚古墳 (桜井市)|version=91584033|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q84638447]] Wikidata name: Kabutozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun Mound (front section on the left, rear circular section in the back right) | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]][[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] Asago (Kabutozuka area) | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|qq=}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JapanKnowledge|qq=}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|兜塚古墳 (桜井市)|version=91584033|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}}

The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q84638447]] Wikidata name: Kabutozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai With carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped stone coffin.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped stone coffin|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped stone coffins. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped Sarcophagus.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|qq=|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped Sarcophagus.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * [[sn:commons:Category:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)]][[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q84638447]] Wikidata name: Kabutozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped Sarcophagus.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese Historical Place name Series|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place name Series|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30: Nara Prefecture Place names|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301}} ** Updated version (included in {{ill|JapanKnowledge|ja|ジャパンナレッジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JapanKnowledge|1=JapanKnowledge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11309996}}, 2006). * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=Shinichi Shimizu|year=1989|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} {{translated page|ja|兜塚古墳 (桜井市)|version=91584033|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:兜塚古墳 (桜井市)]] [[ja:兜塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] {{Commons category|Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai)}} {{wikidata link|Q84638447}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section towards the rear circular section File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section towards the front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai City's Historic Site] (Sakurai City Archaeological Center). == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=兜塚古墳|title={{ill|Japanese But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kabutozuka Kofun | image = File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), funkyu.jpg | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | coordinates = {{coord|34|29|53.87|N|135|51|8.18|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length: 40 m
Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped Sarcophagus.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. Height: 3.5 m (rear and front sections) | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (possibly pit-style lateral entrance chamber)
With carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} inside | burial goods = Ornaments, iron arrowheads, horse gear, [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] | built = Late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | Historic Site = Designated as a Sakurai City Historic Site "Kabutozuka Kofun" | map = Japan Nara | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus - WSW | map_dot_label = Kabutozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Kabutozuka Kofun''' (兜塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It has a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] design and is Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Kabutozuka Kofun (Sakurai), sekkan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} in the rear circular section}}{{small|Made of Aso welded [[:en:Tuff|tuff]], an ancient house-shaped Sarcophagus.}}]] The kofun is constructed at the northwestern tip of a ridge extending west of the Asago settlement in the southern [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It has been subjected to grave robbery, and an excavation was conducted in 1954. The mound is keyhole-shaped, oriented westward. It is built on a plattype, with small gravel (possibly [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]]) and fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} identified on the surface. The burial chamber is a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} located in the center of the rear circular section, aligned parallel to the mound's main axis. It was constructed using flat river stones, measuring approximately 3.7 m in length and 1.4 m in width, though much of it has been destroyed. Some researchers suggest it may be a pit-style lateral entrance chamber due to the presence of a gravel spread on the east side of the stone chamber. Inside the chamber, a carved {{ill|house-shaped Sarcophagus|ja|家形石棺|1=House-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/house-shaped Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} made from Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Umamon stone) rests on a clay bed. The coffin measures about 2.1 m long, 1 m wide, and 1 m high. Its lid is rounded with minimal ridges, featuring two rope-hanging knobs slightly angled upward on each side—a style typical of ancient house-shaped Sarcophaguss. Similar coffins made from Aso welded tuff are also found in kofun like the {{ill|Nogami Kofun|ja|野神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogami Kofun|1=Nogami Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24872950}} in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]]. Although the site was looted, excavations revealed grave goods, including ornaments (jade tubular beads, amber barrel-shaped beads, silver hollow beads, glass beads), iron arrowheads, and horse gear (gilt bronze decorative plates, apricot-leaf-shaped pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the mid-[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. The site was Historic Site1 as a Sakurai City Historic Site in 2000. == Mound area == * Total length: 40 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 20 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters * Front section: ** Width: 19 meters ** Height: 3.5 meters The mound is built on a plattype measuring 48 meters east-west, 26 meters north-south, and 4 meters in height. front section == Historic Site == === Sakurai City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Kabutozuka Kofun - Designated on June 12, 2000.[http://www.sakurai-maibun.nara.jp/sitei.html Sakurai
